subject: Re: Westminster
body:
never heard from the lady


----- Original Message ----
From: Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Sent: Wednesday, October 3, 2007 8:34:03 AM
Subject: FW: Westminster


 
 
From: Noital4u@aol.com [mailto:Noital4u@aol.com] 
Sent: Wednesday, October 03, 2007 8:48 AM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: Westminster
 
Good morning Lesley,
 
Tell me how things worked out with Jeffrey's puppy???  I hope everything was perfect and he's hugging his Springer right now!
Westminster is just around the corner and I am sure "James" will be the center of attention as the reining Best in Show dog.
 
I have some exciting news, I have a Champion Sheltie named "Jamie"  that I am bringing to show at Westminster this year.
I am thrilled.  This will be my first time back to the Garden since my beloved Mocha died.
 
I have a big favor to ask.  I have searched for hotels and during those days of Westminster, everyone I tried is booked solid.  A few years ago I stayed in one of Jeffrey's apartments in the City.  Would you be so kind as to ask him if I and my entourage  (handler and girl friends)  could use his places again?  
Gee, maybe he would like us to stay with him!!!  He'd have a great time with us, doggie and all!!
 
Tell him too, that the Quarter Horse Congress is in full swing.  He should come and go with me as he did in the old days.
 
I have so many really cool things to tell him about.   What's the best, on the phone or should I send him a note?
 
Thanks a million Lesley!
Pamela






See what's new at AOL.com and Make AOL Your Homepage.

Subject: Westminster

 

Good morning Lesley,

 

Tell me how things worked out with Jeffrey's puppy???  I hope everything was perfect and he's hugging his Springer right now!

Westminster is just around the corner and I am sure "James" will be the center of attention as the reining Best in Show dog.

 

I have some exciting news, I have a Champion Sheltie named "Jamie"  that I am bringing to show at Westminster this year.

I am thrilled.  This will be my first time back to the Garden since my beloved Mocha died.

 

I have a big favor to ask.  I have searched for hotels and during those days of Westminster, everyone I tried is booked solid.  A few years ago I stayed in one of Jeffrey's apartments in the City.  Would you be so kind as to ask him if I and my entourage  (handler and girl friends)  could use his places again? 

Gee, maybe he would like us to stay with him!!!  He'd have a great time with us, doggie and all!!

 

Tell him too, that the Quarter Horse Congress is in full swing.  He should come and go with me as he did in the old days.

 

I have so many really cool things to tell him about.   What's the best, on the phone or should I send him a note?

 

Thanks a million Lesley!

Pamela
